etymology.description: phonetic 樊(fán) (fence) + semantic 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") -> climb
etymology.interpretation: 攀(pān) combines the phonetic element 樊(fán) with the semantic 手(手), suggesting the use of hands to climb over fences or obstacles. The hand represents the action of climbing, while 樊 provides the sound.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢去山区攀岩(他喜歡去山區攀岩)。
examples.english: He likes to go rock climbing in the mountains.

examples.chinese: 我们一起攀登这座高山(我們一起攀登這座高山)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yīqǐ pāndēng zhè zuò gāoshān.
examples.english: We will climb this tall mountain together.

examples.chinese: 在春天，他常常攀树(在春天，他常常攀樹)，享受大自然(享受大自然)。
examples.pinyin: Zài chūntiān, tā chángcháng pān shù, xiǎngshòu dàzìrán.
examples.english: In spring, he often climbs trees to enjoy nature.
